What it means: Not applicable - a single instance, so there is nothing to arrange.
Clarify more: The default. Must be an em-dash, never blank: blank would read as 'unknown' rather than 'there is only one instance'.

What it means: Every instance converges on the ONE aim target.
Used by: Akshan (6 shots), Azir (3 Soldiers strike), Gwen (3 snips)
Clarify more: Multiplicity with no geometry - it is a burst of repeats, not a shape. Each instance is a SEPARATE hitbox, so the target is hit once per instance, and one can miss while another lands. Contrast 'Current + Left + Right', whose instances SHARE one hitbox. And do not confuse this with an Amount: Kalista fires ONE projectile that lands 6 Impale stacks, so she is Count 1 - see 'Note - Count vs Amount'.

What it means: The instances fan out in a cone in front of the caster.
Clarify more: The cone is an emergent shape, NOT a hitbox: there is no cone collider. Each arrow is its own First hit Projectile and stops on the first body in ITS lane - which is why a wide cone can still miss. A cone is NEVER a hitbox anywhere in this sheet. The only other thing that looks like one is Gwen's Sweep Laser, and that is a LINE sweeping sideways - the cone is just the shape its path leaves behind. (An earlier version of this row claimed Gwen's cone was a real collider; that was wrong.)

What it means: One instance at the current target, one at the enemy to its LEFT, one to its RIGHT.
Clarify more: Deliberately not three on the same target, which would be overpowered. All three share ONE hitbox, so an enemy caught by two of them is not damaged twice - the opposite of 'Same target'.

What it means: The instances do NOT converge and form no shape - each one goes to its own target.
Aims around: One aim per instance, re-picked as it fires
Clarify more: Named for WHERE the instances go, like every other spread. What is unusual is WHEN the aim is decided: each of Soraka's stars asks again 'which enemy is closest to the healed ally NOW?', and over 5 seconds the board moves, so star 5 can land on a different enemy than star 1. Contrast 'Same target', which fixes one target at cast and sends every instance there regardless. (This was called 'Re-picked per instance' - a WHEN answer wearing a WHERE column's clothes, which is why it never sat right.)

What it means: One instance expanding from the caster in ALL directions at once.
Aims around: Self - it is not aimed at anything
Clarify more: Count stays 1: it is a single expanding hitbox, not N instances. It earns a Spread anyway because the arrangement is what makes it un-aimed - the hitbox is large enough to reach the whole board, so Aim Target = Self and Recipient = All enemies.

What it means: The Count instances are DIVIDED among the N nearest enemies - not all on one, and not re-picked freely.
Clarify more: Distinct from 'Same target' (every instance converges on ONE) and from 'Each to its own target' (each instance re-picks its own aim as it fires). Here a FIXED set of N targets splits a larger pool of instances between them, so each of the N is hit roughly Count/N times.

What it means: The instances fire on DIAGONALS from the ACTION SOURCE - not from the caster.
Used by: Vel'Koz (2 pierce bolts split off his first-hit projectile)
Clarify more: The only spread measured from a PROJECTILE rather than the champion: Vel'Koz's first-hit bolt becomes the action source, and when it lands two pierce projectiles split diagonally off it. Every other spread is arranged around the caster's Aim Target.

Spread: Spread answers one question: given Count instances of a single Action, WHERE do they go?

It is orthogonal to the Action. Ashe (Cone) and Akshan (Same target) fire the exact same Action - First hit Projectile - and differ only in arrangement, which is why Spread is its own tab rather than a property of the Action. The Action's own decomposition lives in 'Action Templates'.

Count = 1 means Spread is an em-dash: a single instance has nothing to arrange. The one exception is 360° radial - a single expanding hitbox whose ARRANGEMENT is still what makes it un-aimed (Aim Target = Self, Recipient = All enemies).
